workaround: avoid SDK cancellation serialization bug (#320)

## Summary

Pass \CancellationToken.None\ to SDK \SendAsync\ calls to prevent
\StreamJsonRpc\'s \StandardCancellationStrategy\ from attempting to
serialize \RequestId\, which is not included in the SDK's AOT-safe JSON
context.

Fixes #319

## Root Cause (SDK Bug)

When a \CancellationToken\ fires during an SDK call:
1. \StreamJsonRpc.StandardCancellationStrategy.CancelOutboundRequest()\
tries to send a \$/cancelRequest\ notification
2. This requires serializing \StreamJsonRpc.RequestId\
3. The SDK's \SystemTextJsonFormatter\ doesn't include \RequestId\ in
its \TypeInfoResolverChain\
4. \NotSupportedException\ is thrown, becomes unobserved, kills the
JSON-RPC reader loop silently
5. Session gets stuck — no events arrive, watchdog eventually fires

## Why This Workaround Works

PolyPilot already handles cancellation at the TCS level:
- \AbortSessionAsync\ calls \ResponseCompletion.TrySetCanceled()\
- Watchdog timeout clears \IsProcessing\ state
- All abort/error paths properly clean up state

The \CancellationToken\ was only used by SDK to send server-side
cancellation notifications — which is the broken path.

## Trade-off

The server won't receive \$/cancelRequest\ notifications, so it may
continue processing cancelled requests. However:
- Local state is correctly cleaned up (good UX)
- Watchdog provides safety net
- Server resources are wasted but operations complete gracefully

## Investigation Summary

4 parallel sub-agents analyzed:

| Agent | Focus | Finding |
|-------|-------|---------|
| SDK changelogs | Breaking changes | None documented for cancellation |
| SDK Client.cs | JsonRpc config | Confirms
\StandardCancellationStrategy\ used, \RequestId\ missing from JSON
context |
| PolyPilot audit | CancellationToken usage | Our usage is correct |
| SDK samples | Recommended patterns | Don't use CancellationToken at
all |

**All agents agree**: This is an SDK bug, not a PolyPilot defect.

## Changes

- **CopilotService.cs**: 2 lines changed
  - Primary \SendAsync\ call (line 2351)
  - Reconnect retry \SendAsync\ call (line 2499)

## Testing

- Build passes
- 139 watchdog tests pass
- Manual testing: orchestrator sessions no longer get stuck on
cancellation

## SDK Versions Affected

- Broken: 0.1.30+ (after AOT/trimming changes)
- Works: 0.1.26

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Replace reference casts with `addr_of!` in `Soul::value_ptr` and `Soul::count_ptr` (#25)

* Initial plan

* fix(soul): replace reference casts with addr_of! in value_ptr and count_ptr

Use `core::ptr::addr_of!` to obtain raw pointers to Soul fields without
forming intermediate shared references, as recommended by the Rust Nomicon.
This avoids a provenance mismatch where a `*mut T` was derived from `&T`.

Fixes issue 17.

Co-authored-by: Magicolo <5423040+Magicolo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use addr_of on self fields and simplify casts

Replace addr_of!((*self.get_ref()).field) with addr_of!(self.field)
and rely on type inference for NonNull::new_unchecked casts. Reflow a
long safety doc comment and tidy ptr imports.

fix: publish native packages via custom pipeline, skip in nx release (#20)

* fix(ci): publish native packages via custom pipeline, skip in nx release

NAPI packages (transform-native, plugin-axum) cannot use OIDC
provenance for first-time publishes because npm OIDC trust is not
configured for newly created packages. Override nx-release-publish
target to no-op for these packages and publish them via the custom
npm-publish.ts script which handles OIDC/token fallback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): skip build/e2e jobs on pull_request_target events

pull_request_target checks out the base branch (main), not the PR
branch. Only lint-pr-title needs this event. Build and e2e jobs
should only run on push and pull_request events.

Add napkin plugin: visual whiteboard collaboration for Copilot CLI (#929)

* Add napkin plugin: visual whiteboard collaboration for Copilot CLI

Napkin opens an interactive HTML whiteboard in the user's browser where
they can draw, sketch, and add sticky notes. When ready, they click
'Share with Copilot' which exports a PNG snapshot. The Copilot CLI agent
reads the PNG via the view tool, and the multimodal AI model interprets
the drawings, spatial layout, and text content — responding
conversationally as a collaborator.

Built for non-technical users: lawyers, PMs, business stakeholders,
designers, and anyone who thinks better visually.

Includes:
- Self-contained HTML whiteboard (zero external dependencies)
- Shape recognition (wobbly shapes snap to clean versions)
- Freehand drawing, sticky notes, arrows, text labels
- Auto-save to localStorage
- SKILL.md with agent instructions
- SVG visual guides for step-by-step README documentation
- Plugin manifest for Copilot CLI installation

commit.message:
Replace webpack with esbuild for extension bundling (#79)

* Replace webpack with esbuild for extension bundling

- Add esbuild.config.mjs as a simple build script
- Add src/content_scripts/index.js entry point (esbuild doesn't merge
  multiple entries into one output like webpack)
- Update compile script in package.json to use esbuild
- Add esbuild.config.mjs to eslint ignores
- Remove webpack and webpack-cli dependencies (removes 92 packages)

* Rename background.js to index.js for consistency

* Consolidate manifest and icon generation into esbuild config

Move manifest generation and icon copying from separate tool scripts
into esbuild.config.mjs so the entire build is a single command.
Remove tools/ directory.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add engines.node >= 18 and update BUILDING.md

esbuild and ESLint v9 both require Node 18+. Add an engines field
to package.json and update BUILDING.md which previously referenced
Node 14.

* Upgrade eslint to v10 and globals to v17

Bump Node.js requirement to >=20 (required by ESLint 10).
Update BUILDING.md accordingly.

* Remove unnecessary version lifecycle script

The addon/ directory is gitignored, so regenerating manifests during
npm version served no purpose. All relevant commands (build, start,
test, lint) already run compile first.

* Run esbuild builds in parallel

The Chrome and Firefox builds are independent, so run them
concurrently with Promise.all instead of sequentially.

- Rename all src/**/*.js to .ts with full type annotations
- Add tsconfig.json with strict mode

commit.message:
fix: use relative date in profile streak test to prevent monthly flakiness (#74)

The test used the 1st of the current month for a group workout date,
but the streak calculator resets at 7+ day gaps. After the 8th of any
month, the gap exceeded tolerance causing highestStreakEver to be 1
instead of 2. Use yesterday instead, which is always within tolerance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: widen ReconnectState test window to fix CI failure (#321)

## CI Failure

`ReconnectState_ShouldCarryIsMultiAgentSession` fails in CI
([run](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/22836953792/job/66235226971))
with:

```
Assert.Contains() Failure: Sub-string not found
String:    "newState.HasUsedToolsThisTurn = state.Has"···
Not found: "newState.IsMultiAgentSession = state.IsMu"···
```

## Root Cause

This source-scanning test checks that `IsMultiAgentSession` is carried
forward near `HasUsedToolsThisTurn` in the reconnect block of
`SendPromptAsync`. The test uses a 200-char search window, but the
`SuccessfulToolCountThisTurn` carry-forward line (added in a later PR
between the two) pushed `IsMultiAgentSession` beyond that window.

The production code is correct — `IsMultiAgentSession` IS properly
carried forward at line 2471.

## Fix

Widened the search window from 200 → 400 chars to accommodate
intermediate carry-forward lines.

commit.message:
feat: incremental sync and GitHub webhook support for Git repositories (#238)

* Initial plan

* feat: incremental sync and webhook-triggered sync for Git repositories

Phase 1: Incremental Sync
- Add LastHeadCommitSha to GitRepository model
- Add IncrementalSyncResult record and IncrementalSyncRepositoryAsync to IGitRepositoryService
- Implement incremental sync using GitHub Compare API in GitRepositoryService
  - Falls back to full sync when no prior SHA or diff >= 250 files
  - Handles added/modified/renamed/removed files
- Update SyncRepository and CreateRepository controller actions
- Add EF Core migration for LastHeadCommitSha

Phase 2: Webhook-Triggered Sync
- Add POST /api/webhooks/github endpoint in new WebhooksController
- HMAC-SHA256 signature validation with constant-time comparison
- Parse push events and trigger incremental sync for matching repositories
- Add GitHub:WebhookSecret config placeholder in appsettings.json
- Skip auth middleware for /api/webhooks/* paths

Tests: WebhooksControllerTests, GitRepositoryServiceTests (31 tests passing)

Co-authored-by: chz160 <3420276+chz160@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address all PR review comments on incremental sync and webhook

- ValidateSignature: wrap Convert.FromHexString in try-catch FormatException
  so invalid hex header returns false instead of 500
- DRY: extract SyncResultExtensions.ApplyIncrementalSyncResult shared helper;
  remove duplicated add/update/delete blocks in both controllers
- Remove ParseFullName duplication from WebhooksController; expose
  GitRepositoryService.ParseGitHubUrl as internal static and reuse it
- Webhook repo query: pre-filter with Contains before materializing to
  avoid loading all repos into memory
- Full-sync fallback: add IsFullSync flag to IncrementalSyncResult;
  ApplyIncrementalSyncResult removes all existing files on full sync
  (prevents duplicate rows) vs diff-only on incremental sync
- IsIncremental in sync response now reflects !syncResult.IsFullSync
- Fix subdirectory file paths in FetchQueryFileAsync: remove
  Uri.EscapeDataString on path (forward slashes must stay unencoded)
- Add tests for FormatException path and IsFullSync flag (34 tests passing)

Co-authored-by: chz160 <3420276+chz160@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply remaining PR review comments

- CreateRepository: replace manual foreach loop with ApplyIncrementalSyncResult
  to use the shared extension method consistently with SyncRepository/webhook
- ValidateSignature: remove unnecessary Convert.FromHexString(Convert.ToHexString(...))
  round-trip; use HMACSHA256.HashData result bytes directly

Co-authored-by: chz160 <3420276+chz160@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add refresh_seconds configuration to Dad Jokes plugin (#367)

* Initial plan

* Add refresh_seconds config to Dad Jokes plugin with min/max validation and caching

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor validate_config to check type once before range validation

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move refresh interval support from Dad Jokes into generic PluginBase

Refactored the refresh_seconds / caching feature so it is available
to ALL plugins via the base class, not just Dad Jokes.

PluginBase changes (src/plugins/base.py):
- Added get_data() method with automatic time-based caching
- Added refresh_seconds property (reads from manifest settings_schema)
- Added clear_cache() method
- Added _validate_refresh_seconds() for manifest-driven validation
- Cache auto-clears on config change or plugin disable

PluginRegistry changes (src/plugins/registry.py):
- fetch_plugin_data() now calls plugin.get_data() (cached) instead
  of plugin.fetch_data() (always fresh)
- set_plugin_config() runs base refresh_seconds validation alongside
  plugin-specific validate_config()

Plugin cleanups:
- dad_jokes: removed plugin-specific caching, validation, and constants;
  fetch_data() is now a pure API call, caching handled by base
- spacecraft_launches: removed refresh_seconds validation (base handles it)
- sports_scores: removed refresh_seconds validation (base handles it)
- wsdot: removed refresh_seconds validation (base handles it)
- _template: removed manual refresh_seconds validation example

How it works for any plugin:
- If a plugin's manifest.json has refresh_seconds in settings_schema,
  get_data() automatically caches results for that interval
- If no refresh_seconds in manifest, get_data() delegates directly
  to fetch_data() with no caching (backwards compatible)
- Validation min/max bounds are read from the manifest schema

Tests: 31 new base class tests + updated plugin tests (259 total pass)

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add hard rate-limit floor (min_refresh_seconds) to prevent 429s

Adds a non-user-configurable rate-limit floor that is enforced at
runtime, regardless of how plugin config is loaded or set.

Manifest changes:
- New top-level 'min_refresh_seconds' field added to all 17 plugin
  manifests that define refresh_seconds.  This is the absolute minimum
  interval a plugin will ever use, set by the plugin developer.

PluginBase changes (src/plugins/base.py):
- New 'min_refresh_seconds' property: reads the manifest field first,
  falls back to settings_schema minimum, then global MIN_REFRESH_SECONDS
- 'refresh_seconds' property now clamps to the floor at runtime -- even
  if stored config somehow contains a lower value, the plugin will never
  poll faster than min_refresh_seconds
- '_validate_refresh_seconds' uses the same floor for config validation

CI validation (tests/test_plugin_validation.py):
- min_refresh_seconds must be a positive integer
- min_refresh_seconds must be <= settings_schema minimum (consistency)
- Plugins with refresh_seconds must declare min_refresh_seconds

Tests: 13 new tests covering the floor property, runtime clamping,
       fallback chain, and manifest consistency (all pass)

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CI: relax rate-limit floor test to accept schema minimum as fallback

The test_plugins_with_refresh_seconds_have_floor test strictly required
an explicit min_refresh_seconds field in every plugin manifest. This
broke when the generic_data plugin (added to main after our branch
forked) has refresh_seconds with a schema minimum but no explicit
min_refresh_seconds.

PluginBase already falls back to the schema minimum at runtime, so the
test now accepts either an explicit min_refresh_seconds OR a minimum in
the settings_schema refresh_seconds property.

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

workoutGoal, unitPreference, and healthProfilePromptDismissed from user data.
Applied to GET /users/:uid, GET /users/:uid/profile, and GET /users/search

* feat: US-005 - Add Health & Body section with unit preference to account settings

Add Health & Body section to the account settings page with:
- Unit preference toggle (metric/imperial) with auto-detection from navigator.language
- Height input: cm for metric, feet+inches for imperial with conversion
- Weight input: kg for metric, lbs for imperial with conversion
- Date of birth input with computed age display
- Privacy indicator (lock icon + 'Private — only visible to you')
- All fields save on blur via PUT /users/me mutation
- Follows existing React Query mutation pattern

* feat: US-006 - Add Workout Goal selector to account settings

Add Workout Goal section below Health & Body with:
- 5 goal options as selectable cards in responsive grid (2 cols, 3 on sm+)
- Uses WORKOUT_GOAL_LABELS from @burnbuddy/shared for emoji and label text
- Selected goal highlighted with primary color border/background/ring
- Click to select, click again to deselect (sends null to clear)
- Shows current goal status text or 'No goal selected'
- Saves immediately via healthFieldMutation (PUT /users/me)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-007 - Add onboarding health profile prompt for new users

Add HealthProfilePrompt component shown on dashboard when
healthProfilePromptDismissed is falsy. Contains:
- Unit preference toggle (auto-detected from navigator.language)
- Height input (metric/imperial)
- Weight input (metric/imperial)
- Date of birth input
- Workout goal selector cards
- Skip button (dismisses prompt only)
- Save & Continue button (saves all fields + dismisses)

Also adds 'Complete your health profile' banner to account settings
when prompt was dismissed but no health fields are set.
